task: Launch-quality advanced task: UPS Autonomy Capacity Verification. Reality context: a data center must verify UPS autonomy after battery replacements. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=load_kW, metric2=battery_string_health_pct, metric3=runtime_min, metric4=reserve_margin_min; records: R1 load_kW=854.8 battery_string_health_pct=59.8 runtime_min=129 reserve_margin_min=237; R2 load_kW=248.2 battery_string_health_pct=73.2 runtime_min=58 reserve_margin_min=87; R3 load_kW=649.7 battery_string_health_pct=44.8 runtime_min=228 reserve_margin_min=211; R4 load_kW=305.6 battery_string_health_pct=19.1 runtime_min=207 reserve_margin_min=46; limit_load_kW=924.9; limit_battery_string_health_pct=62; limit_runtime_min=176; decision_budget_usd=137500; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="challenge required self-contained inputs and objectively checkable scoring". Rules and constraints: Compute margin = metric4 - (0.35*metric1 + 0.25*metric2 + 0.15*metric3); negative margin is fail and margin below 8% of the largest driver is warn. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: confirm runtime and weak strings; build a reproducible workbook or script, preserve intermediate calculations, and produce an expert-review memo. Deliver ups_autonomy_check.xlsx + maintenance_note.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
